title: HTTP Semantics, Messages, Versions, and the Wire Model · Go Build Room
summary: A protocol pairing room predicts byte, parser, state, ownership, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first version-neutral message and semantics model for http semantics, messages, versions, and the wire model. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of version-neutral message and semantics model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate version-neutral method, status, field, content, and resource semantics from HTTP/1.1 textual serialization.
Model control data, header fields, content, trailer fields, and message metadata without inventing content where semantics forbid it.
Determine content meaning and permission from request method, response status, request context, and connection mode rather than one field alone.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Semantics Syntax Separation
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop protocol team handles case 2806 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive message content from method and status semantics before wire framing; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then append a second pipelined message and prove the first parser leaves the exact residual bytes. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate version-neutral method, status, field, content, and resource semantics from HTTP/1.1 textual serialization.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Semantics Syntax Separation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate version-neutral method, status, field, content, and resource semantics from http/1.1 textual serialization. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Semantics Syntax Separation, keep Semantics Syntax Separation connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
